section_name: Speed Limit Exception for Emergencies; Municipal Regulation
text:
(a) The regulation of the speed of a vehicle under this subchapter does not apply to:

(1) an authorized emergency vehicle responding to a call;

(2) a police patrol; or

(3) a physician or ambulance responding to an emergency call.

(b) A municipality by ordinance may regulate the speed of:

(1) an ambulance;

(2) an emergency medical services vehicle; or

(3) an authorized vehicle operated by a blood or tissue bank.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.365 — Speed Limit Exception for Emergencies; Municipal Regulation

(a) The regulation of the speed of a vehicle under this subchapter does not apply to:

    (1) an authorized emergency vehicle responding to a call;

    (2) a police patrol; or

    (3) a physician or ambulance responding to an emergency call.

(b) A municipality by ordinance may regulate the speed of:

    (1) an ambulance;

    (2) an emergency medical services vehicle; or

    (3) an authorized vehicle operated by a blood or tissue bank.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The regulation of the speed of a vehicle under this subchapter does not apply to:
an authorized emergency vehicle responding to a call;
a physician or ambulance responding to an emergency call.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A municipality by ordinance may regulate the speed of:
an emergency medical services vehicle; or
an authorized vehicle operated by a blood or tissue bank.
